critic_raw:
Assessor A: The evaluation's headline metric (AUC) does not align with the business decision (who to include in the top 2% call list). This is a well-known failure mode in constrained intervention settings. The paper should report precision and recall at the exact 2% threshold, and ideally lift relative to random selection. Neither is provided. Additionally, the incremental benefit to the retention program — fewer churners among called users — is not measured. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor B: From a statistical perspective, global AUC and top-k precision are related but not equivalent. The relationship is particularly weak under high class imbalance (6% churn prevalence here). At 6% prevalence, ROC-AUC is more sensitive to performance on the majority class (non-churners). Improvements in separating low-risk from medium-risk non-churners can boost AUC without improving the top-2% list composition. The paper should have used precision-recall AUC or reported top-k metrics. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor C: The retention team's business question is: 'Will this model help us call the right people?' The paper answers: 'This model ranks users better overall.' These are related but not identical. Better overall ranking does not guarantee better selection at the top 2%, especially when the model improvement may be concentrated in the lower deciles where intervention is impossible anyway. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Synthesis: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed. The ETD: compute top-2% precision, recall, and lift from the existing test month data for both models.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous across three assessors. All identify AUC-threshold misalignment and absent capacity-constrained metrics. Assessor B adds a useful statistical point about AUC behavior under class imbalance. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.measure: precision@2%, recall@2%, and lift@2% on held-out test month for both models
empirical_test.success_criterion: new model precision and lift at 2% cutoff are materially better than incumbent, confirming the AUC improvement translates to the operational threshold
empirical_test.failure_criterion: top-2% precision and lift are comparable or worse for the new model despite higher AUC, indicating the improvement did not concentrate at the relevant operating point
